source_label: BIP44 — Multi-Account Hierarchy for Deterministic Wallets
excerpt: Defines the path m / purpose' / coin_type' / account' / change / address_index. Adopted with purpose=84' for native segwit (BIP84) and purpose=86' for taproot (BIP86). The shared convention is what allows different wallet implementations to discover each other's funds from the same seed.
body:
Source excerpt:
Defines the path m / purpose' / coin_type' / account' / change / address_index. Adopted with purpose=84' for native segwit (BIP84) and purpose=86' for taproot (BIP86). The shared convention is what allows different wallet implementations to discover each other's funds from the same seed.

---

Cited by leaf "HD wallets and BIP32 derivation":
An HD (hierarchical deterministic) wallet derives every one of its keys from a single seed via a deterministic tree. From the seed you compute a master extended key; from that master, child keys at any path you like; from any child, more children, indefinitely [1]. The practical consequence: backing up the seed backs up the entire wallet — current keys, future keys, every account you'll ever derive from it — without needing to copy out individual private keys ever.

The derivation path you see on wallet screens — usually written `m/84'/0'/0'/0/5` or similar — encodes which branch of the tree the key lives on. The BIP44/49/84/86 conventions assign meaning to the first few levels: purpose (script type — 84' is native segwit, 86' is taproot), coin type (0' is Bitcoin), account index, change-or-receive, and address index [2]. Two wallets that agree on those conventions can both sweep the same seed and find the same coins; that's why "recover seed in any BIP39-compatible wallet" works in practice.

The `'` mark means the derivation step is hardened: the parent's private key is required to derive the child. Without hardening, the math allows a third party with the parent xpub plus any child private key to compute the parent private key — fine inside a single wallet that controls everything, catastrophic if you've shared an xpub for watch-only purposes [3]. This is why all account-level paths are hardened: it lets you safely hand out an account xpub for receive-address generation by another device without exposing the master.
